messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\n\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS\nPrevious validation issues:\nNone",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "initialize-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and basic files",
      "description": "Create project directory, run npm init, add .gitignore, setup basic folder structure (src, public, styles), and add a README with goals.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder contains package.json, .gitignore, README, and folders src, public, styles; npm init completed without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install Express, Tailwind build tools and dev utilities",
      "description": "Install express and dev tools (nodemon), and Tailwind-related packages (tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer) and add useful npm scripts for dev and build.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "package.json lists express, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer and dev dependencies; npm scripts include start, dev, and build commands."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind and CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Initialize tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, create input CSS with tailwind directives, and add build script that outputs compiled CSS into public directory.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist, compiled CSS file is generated in public/ after running the build script and contains Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Build Express server to serve static frontend and expose color API",
      "description": "Implement server in src/server.js that serves public/ static files, listens on configurable port, and exposes GET /api/color and POST /api/color endpoints to read/write color settings.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies",
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts, serves index.html at /, and responds to GET /api/color and POST /api/color with appropriate JSON (200 on success)."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Create frontend UI with Hello World text and color controls",
      "description": "Implement index.html and small client JS in public/ that displays the Hello World text, a color picker or preset color buttons, updates the text color live using Tailwind-compatible classes or inline styles, and calls the API to save selection.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "User can open the page, change the Hello World color via UI controls, see the change immediately, and client sends the selected color to POST /api/color."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-api-persistence",
      "title": "Persist selected color on the server",
      "description": "Add simple persistence (either in-memory with optional JSON file) so the selected color survives server restarts or is available to clients; ensure GET /api/color returns current persisted value.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "After selecting a color in the UI and reloading the page or restarting the server, GET /api/color returns the chosen color and the UI initializes with that color."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-run",
      "title": "Test end-to-end and document run instructions",
      "description": "Run development build, verify Tailwind build, start Express server, test color change flows, and document commands for dev and production start in README.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-ui",
        "implement-api-persistence",
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Manual test passes: page loads, color can be changed and persists, API endpoints function; README contains clear start/build commands."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Layered functional decomposition: start with project initialization and dependency installation, configure styling build (Tailwind), implement server API and static serving, build frontend UI that interacts with API, add persistence, and finish with testing and documentation."
}
